ref: Jacob loved Joseph more than he did any of his other sons, because Joseph was born when Jacob was very old. Jacob had even given Joseph a fancy coat
text: Dɛ Josef ɛ Jekɔb banjenbie, anomanta, dɛ o dɛ nyin o waa ɡwaa o bii la bwa. Asɛ o nyarɛ batakaarejaŋ ondɛɛɡe bɛl baa di ɡwini dawɛ o ta tɛo.
translated: Josef and Jacob are brothers, twins, and they do not know that they are in the same village. But they will meet at the market and then they will know that they are brothers.

ref: At this time Abram the Hebrew was living near the oaks that belonged to Mamre the Amorite. Mamre and his brothers Eshcol and Aner were Abram's friends. Someone who had escaped from the battle told Abram
text: A habɔɔ la, nɛra baa chwaa la bini bɛl ba Abram waa ɛ Hibru te la be, dɛ ba bol ɡɛ waa la o be. Dɛ o soɡe Mamre dajena ane aa lɔ Amɔre te bɛl harɛ ta la mune. Dɛ Mamre ne o nimanar Ɛskɔl ne Aner bwa ja Abram nɛ baa cha dɔŋa laa ta.
translated: At that time, the Canaanites and the Perizzites were dwelling in the land where Abram was staying as a foreigner, and he was still traveling through the land. Then he came to the oak of Mamre, which is in Hebron, and pitched his tent there. The owners of the land, Mamre, Eshcol, and Aner, were allies of Abram and helped him in battle.

ref: Then God's angel called me by name. I answered,
text: Belaa nyundua torome bɛl yer mɛ deera la ta dɛ ŋɔ, ‘Jekɔb’ asɛ n sɛh ŋɔ ‘Menee nɔ.’
translated: When the man saw that he could not overpower him, he touched the socket of his hip so that the socket of Jacob's hip was out of joint as he wrestled with him.

ref: At sunset he stopped for the night and went to sleep, resting his head on a large rock.
text: Waa dɛ kaa la, o kaa tel bobɛl bɔr joo o chɔ, dɛ kpɔ nyu serɛ bwe bɛl.
translated: They will go to the farm, he will carry a basket on his head and go, they will dig up new yams and bring them home.

ref: She was having a rough time, but the woman who was helping her said, “Don't worry! It's a boy.”
text: O lola bini aa dɛ do kpeɡri la, haah waa dɛ lɛo lol la ŋɔ o be ŋɔ, “Bɛ ɔmɛ, bibaalee hɛɛ ba lola ja.”
translated: The woman who was going to the farm said to her co-wife, "Come, let's go to the farm together."
